zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert III de Beke, Knight於1297年6月12日出生在英國斯塔福爾霍普頓時，他的父親Sir Robert II de Draycote Beke27歲，母親Elizabeth FitzHerbert19歲。他於1313年與Mary Stafford在英國英格蘭結婚。他去世於1346年10月12日在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾斯皮爾斯比艾雷斯比享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert III de Beke, Knight于1297年6月12日出生在英国斯塔福爾霍普頓时，他的父亲Sir Robert II de Draycote Beke27岁，母亲Elizabeth FitzHerbert19岁。他于1313年与Mary Stafford在英国英格兰结婚。他去世于1346年10月12日在英国英格兰林可爾史爾斯皮爾斯比艾雷斯比享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beke,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: variant of   Beek  .
Hungarian (also Béke): most probably from a Hungarian form of the Turkish status name beg (see Beg ). Alternatively, it may be a pet form of a Hungarian personal name such as Benedek .

variants.name: Beek
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ernst, Johannes, Kurt, Otto.

Dutch and North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a stream or creek, beek (compare Bek 6), or a habitational name from any of various Dutch places called Beek (in Limburg, Gelderland, and North Brabant), named with the same word.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert III de Beke, Knight was born on 12 June 1297, in Hopton, Staffordshire, England, his father, Sir Robert II de Draycote Beke, was 27 and his mother, Elizabeth FitzHerbert, was 19. He married Mary Stafford in 1313, in England, United Kingdom. He died on 12 October 1346, in Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

Il a épousé Mary Stafford en 1313, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 12 octobre 1346, à Eresby, Spilsby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert III de Beke, Knight(이)가 1297년 6월 12일 영국 스태퍼드실르 홉턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Robert II de Draycote Beke(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth FitzHerbert(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1313년 영국 잉글랜드에서 Mary Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1346년 10월 12일 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스피스비 레스비에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert III de Beke, Knight родился 12 июня 1297 г. в местности хоптон, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Robert II de Draycote Beke было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth FitzHerbert, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Stafford в 1313 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 12 октября 1346 г. в местности эресби, спилсби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.
